configs.description: Maximum raw provider response accepted before parsing (all providers).
configs.name: AI_MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES

configs.description: --- Multi-provider AI routing (all optional; see docs/provider-routing.md) --- Each step may carry a cross-provider route chain of comma-separated `provider:model` tokens (bare model = gemini:<model>). An explicit route REPLACES the step's GEMINI_* chain; empty = Gemini-only behavior above. Known providers: gemini, openai, deepseek, qwen, kimi, glm. Example: AI_ROUTE_TRANSLATION=deepseek:deepseek-v4-flash,gemini-3.1-flash-lite
configs.name: AI_ROUTE_EXTRACTION

configs.description: Shadow mode: sampled, metrics-only comparison runs; never affects results.
configs.name: AI_SHADOW_ENABLED

configs.description: Shadow runs are text-only; extraction images are never duplicated to shadows.
configs.name: AI_SHADOW_TIMEOUT_MS

configs.description: Watchdog: an analysis (and a queued waiter) is aborted after this long so a stuck provider call never holds a slot forever.
configs.name: ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT_MS

configs.description: Comma-separated ordered clamd hosts. The adapter tries each in turn and caches the first reachable one, so the same value works whether the API runs inside docker-compose (`clamav`) or on your host (`127.0.0.1`) against a ClamAV container started with: docker compose --profile clamav up -d clamav (port 3310).
configs.name: CLAMAV_HOSTS

configs.description: --- CORS --- Comma-separated list of allowed web origins for the API.
configs.name: CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS

configs.description: --- Optional virus scanning --- When true in production, a failing/unreachable ClamAV fails CLOSED (uploads rejected).
configs.name: ENABLE_CLAMAV

configs.description: --- API docs --- Swagger UI at /docs. Defaults to enabled everywhere except production.
configs.name: ENABLE_SWAGGER

configs.description: --- Gemini (backend only — NEVER expose to the frontend) ---
configs.name: GEMINI_API_KEY

configs.description: Model id is loaded from env. Never hardcoded. Example: gemini-2.5-flash This global chain is the safety net: any pipeline step WITHOUT its own per-step variables below uses this primary + fallbacks.
configs.name: GEMINI_MODEL

configs.description: --- Per-step Gemini model chains (all optional) --- Each pipeline step may pin its own primary + fallbacks so the hardest steps run on the strongest models and cheap mechanical steps run on the fastest/cheapest. Semantics: a step's chain is EXACTLY what its two vars configure (they replace, not merge with, the global chain); leave both empty to use the global chain for that step.  Extraction (hardest: vision + 221-field structured JSON; a weak extraction silently poisons everything downstream — power-first fallbacks, no lite tiers).
configs.name: GEMINI_MODEL_EXTRACTION

configs.description: Candidate generation (recall breadth; the judge verifies its output, so a cheaper-but-smart first fallback is safe).
configs.name: GEMINI_MODEL_GENERATION

configs.description: Judge (final scores + safety; nothing checks the judge's work — power-first fallbacks, no lite tiers).
configs.name: GEMINI_MODEL_JUDGE

configs.description: Result translation (easy text-only step; the backend restores every canonical field afterwards — fastest/cheapest models win here).
configs.name: GEMINI_MODEL_TRANSLATION

configs.description: Idle (inter-chunk) timeout for STREAMING calls: the stream is aborted only after this long with no new token, so a long generation is never cut off while the model is still producing. Used by the /game/analyze/stream route.
configs.name: GEMINI_STREAM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS

configs.description: Total deadline for a single non-streaming Gemini call.
configs.name: GEMINI_TIMEOUT_MS

configs.description: --- Logging --- One of: fatal | error | warn | info | debug | trace
configs.name: LOG_LEVEL

configs.description: One active analysis per browser tab (the client aborts a prior run before starting a new one); a spamming tab is queued then rejected.
configs.name: MAX_ACTIVE_ANALYSES_PER_TAB

configs.description: --- Streaming concurrency / overload protection --- Hard caps on concurrent streaming analyses. Over-capacity runs queue up to MAX_ANALYSIS_QUEUE_SIZE, then are rejected in-band with SERVER_BUSY so a burst can never exhaust memory or the model quota.
configs.name: MAX_GLOBAL_ACTIVE_ANALYSES

configs.description: --- Upload limits ---
configs.name: MAX_IMAGE_SIZE_BYTES

configs.description: --- Frontend (public, safe values only) ---
configs.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV

configs.description: Display-only price on the payment step (mirror the server PAYMENT_PRICE_* above).
configs.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_PAYMENT_PRICE_VALUE

configs.description: Public PayPal client id for the browser Buttons SDK (safe to expose; enables the frontend payment step). Leave blank to keep the free UI.
configs.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID

configs.description: PayPal.me handle for the voluntary "Support Twinzy" link (letters/digits only, max 50). Leave empty to hide the donate link entirely. This renders a plain outbound https://paypal.me/<handle> link and never gates anything. Paid gating exists ONLY through the env-gated PayPal Orders v2 paywall above, per the recorded owner decision (docs/features/paypal-donations-and-paid-results/ 22-go-no-go.md); blank PAYPAL_* credentials keep the game fully free.
configs.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_PAYPAL_ME_USERNAME

configs.description: --- Runtime ---
configs.name: NODE_ENV

configs.description: FAIL-CLOSED image capability: extraction is the ONLY photo-carrying step and always routes through Gemini. Generation, judge, and translation are text-only. A provider is ENABLED iff its API key is set (remove the key to disable). Keys are backend-only; never expose them to the frontend.
configs.name: OPENAI_API_KEY

configs.description: Server-authoritative price (clients can never change it). Dot-decimal + ISO currency. Must mirror NEXT_PUBLIC_PAYMENT_PRICE_* below (checked by npm run knowledge:contradictions).
configs.name: PAYMENT_PRICE_VALUE

configs.description: --- Paid analysis (PayPal Orders v2) — OPTIONAL --- Leave BOTH blank to keep the game free (no payment code runs). Set BOTH to a PayPal REST app.s credentials to require a $0.50 payment before each analysis. SANDBOX first: create the app at developer.paypal.com (Apps & Credentials). Server-side secrets — NEVER prefixed NEXT_PUBLIC and never committed.
configs.name: PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID

configs.description: sandbox | live — which credentials the values above belong to. Keep sandbox until every LIVE condition in docs/features/paypal-donations-and-paid-results/ 22-go-no-go.md is recorded as met.
configs.name: PAYPAL_ENV

configs.description: --- Rate limiting --- Global limit: RATE_LIMIT_MAX requests per RATE_LIMIT_TTL_MS window per client.
configs.name: RATE_LIMIT_TTL_MS

configs.description: Memory is the only built driver. A future Redis/Valkey adapter must land before any driver-selection configuration is introduced. Per-record and total caps so the cache can never grow unbounded.
configs.name: SHARE_RESULT_MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES

configs.description: Public web origin used to build the /share/<uuid> link (server config only, never user input). Point this at your deployed frontend origin in production.
configs.name: SHARE_RESULT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL

configs.description: --- Temporary shareable-result cache (no database; TTL-only) --- A shared result is cached ONLY in memory (or a future Redis/Valkey driver) and auto-expires. There is no database and no image is ever stored. Default TTL is 10 minutes (min 60s, max 3600s). The record is unreachable the instant it expires; the in-memory cache also clears on API restart/redeploy.
configs.name: SHARE_RESULT_TTL_SECONDS

configs.description: Safety-net TTL for orphaned stream-registry entries. Keep above ANALYSIS_TIMEOUT_MS so healthy runs always finish before reclamation.
configs.name: STREAM_TTL_MS

configs.description: Set true only behind a trusted reverse proxy that overwrites forwarding headers.
configs.name: TRUST_PROXY
